name: rdkit
tagline: RDKit cheminformatics reference: molecule I/O, descriptors, fingerprints, substructure search, reactions, 2D/3D generation, visualization.
verdict: Clean, well-scoped RDKit reference skill: clear trigger/negative-trigger guidance, no network egress or credential access, an explicit pickle-deserialization warning shows security awareness, and versioned metadata with documented breaking changes. Main issue is length (833 lines, well over the ~500-line budget), which bloats context but introduces no safety

highlights.summary: As a reference/how-to skill it shows return types informally (e.g. dict from CalcMolDescriptors, PIL image from MolToImage) but never states a formal output contract for what the agent should hand bac
833 lines of dense reference content will consume significant context when loaded in full; only the references/ and scripts/ bundled files are deferred, the SKILL.md body itself is large.
File is 833 lines, well over the ~500-line budget; content is dense reference material rather than obvious padding, but still exceeds the target substantially.
